entry.title: Gothic 1 Remake - Version 1.0.2 - Hotfix
entry.content: — Fixed interaction with Xardas' Demon not working. — Improved savegame/profile logic. — Added functionality to recover savegame profiles in case they are still there but not shown in the game. — Fixed crash after interacting with a document that has pictures. — Fix for Baal Lukor not dying in the Orc Cemetery for some players. — Fixed an issue with crimes when using magic where the wrong instigator got assigned. — Fixed relationships in the Free Mine raid.

They're giving prospective content creators the opportunity to have their videos shown to thousands of people, as well as be reviewed by a panel of established and accomplished TF2 YouTubers! All submissions will receive feedback and constructive criticism. Beginners and veterans are equally welcome! All entries must be submitted before July 15th, 2026 ! entry.title: I usually skip soulslikes not made by FromSoftware, but one Steam demo just won me over
entry.content: I'm a FromSoft fiend, but there are hardly any other soulslikes I can stand. I can dig something a bit more removed like the first-person Lunacid or 2D Mina the Hollower , but there's an uncanny valley effect for me with stuff like Lies of P or Nioh: It's so close to being the thing I love, but doesn't quite bring it home... Read more.

entry.title: Path of Exile 2 Wallpapers
entry.content: Path of Exile 2 loading screens feature artwork created by the talented artists on our team. We've gathered a collection of high-resolution loading screen artwork from Acts One through Four, along with the art for each Ascendancy Class and artwork from other content, that you can use as desktop wallpapers. We've put together versions of these wallpapers in 1920x1080, 2560x1040, and 3840x2160, all of which you can download here .

entry.title: The arcade meets the hex grid in R-Type Tactics—an unlikely fusion that shines like a star on PC
entry.content: Every long-running series benefits from an occasional change of genre. Yakuza dabbling in RPG-style combat works well. Dragon Quest going Dynasty Warriors for a bit is nothing but refreshing. Sonic racing about in a car makes a fun change of pace. Now it's R-Type's turn, the legendary arcade shmup series putting on a strategy hat and stuffing its UI with statistics and material costs... Read more.

entry.title: Patch 1.6.0f1 - Summer Solstice
entry.content: This patch can affect some custom maps, but loading of old maps and savegames is supported. Gameplay:​ Fixed a bug where citizens would always travel to the shop with the most stock, completely ignoring distance. Households now re-evaluate moving home less often, improving performance in large cities. Tourists, commuters, and vacationers now walk along highway shoulders less often when entering or leaving the city. Fixed citizens taking absurdly long trips across the entire map. Pathfinding limi

entry.title: One of the best ways to play Morrowind just got better with improved scripting support for magic mods and a host of crash fixes
entry.content: Morrowind, the gloomiest and mushroomiest of all the Elder Scrolls games, is getting on in years—and it was never the most polished game in town, even upon its release a whopping 24 years ago. If you're sick of those rough edges, you need look no further than OpenMW, a fanmade open-source engine which makes the game play nice with modern hardware and operating systems. If that sounds like your thing, you'll be pleased to know it just got a huge update ... Read more.

Find a player with the pet bonus you want most. This can be ANY pet, including Bundle exclusive pets! 3. Double-click said player, and click "Trade". You do NOT need a pet to do this, you'll find the "Use" button once this Trade Window is up. Do not "trade" your pet itself, that is a separate system... 4. Enjoy your new bonus, forever! Note t

entry.title: Bryce Clark, game director of Poppy Playtime, has played 23,000 hours of Steam games: 'I'm a bit obsessed with learning the market'
entry.content: Bryce Clark, game director on Poppy Playtime, grew up playing DOS games on his family PC. His favourite childhood game was the 1994 fantasy RTS Dominus , which had unfortunate ramifications for his father. "It was nearly impossible to get working," Clark recalls. "My poor dad ended up spending so many hours on the hard line trying to call tech support. 'Why is this videogame not working for my son?' It was a lot about troubleshooting way back.".. Read more.

entry.title: CD Projekt Red boss believes some fans were forever burned by Cyberpunk 2077's disastrous launch: 'I'm convinced that we lost the faith of some people indefinitely'
entry.content: The infamous launch of Cyberpunk 2077 was a low point in CD Projekt Red's history. With hype levels at critical mass, the RPG released in a buggy, unoptimised, and downright unfinished state that was so bad the PS4 version of the game was pulled from the PlayStation store . The company's share price tanked, investors filed a class action lawsuit, and CDPR's reputation was left in tatters... Read more.

entry.content: Following one of the most unforgettable days in Major playoff history, Saturday's Semifinals left two teams standing in pursuit of a Major Championship. After losing the opening map of their Playoffs, FURIA have been unbeaten, rallying back against 9z Team to win 2-1 in the Quarterfinals and taking down Aurora Gaming 2-0 in the Semifinals to reach the Grand Finals for the first time in the organization's history. entry.title: This GTA 5 filmmaker is livestreaming every step of machinima production, from location scouting to live debugging when the game inevitably crashes
entry.content: I've spent an inordinate amount of my life watching Grand Theft Auto machinima over the last decade. And while I can certainly appreciate the amount of editing and effort that goes into each video's production—I don't always think of that toil when gawping at the finished article. According to filmmaker Jordy Veenstra , however, that polished end result only tells half the story... Read more.

entry.title: The Adventures of Elliot: The Millennium Tales review
entry.content: I always thought history was boring as a kid. Nuts to the past, I want cool tech, body mods, and lightspeed travel! But maybe I would have felt differently if I had a game like The Adventures of Elliot: The Millennium Tales back then. What better way is there to appreciate the past than violently cutting down a million monsters in a ye olde fantasy Philadelphia?.. Read more.

entry.title: Account Transfer
entry.content: Captains! The account transfer testing period has come to an end. Thank you to everyone who helped us test the system and shared their feedback. The account transfer feature is now available to all players. To use it, open: My Profile → Account Transfer Then carefully read the pop-up window that appears and review the list of resources that will be transferred. If you encounter any issues or have questions during the transfer process, please contact Support. Fair winds and following seas!

entry.title: Anima Strike Meta Event
entry.content: Anima Strike Meta Event Anima Strike is a three-week event that unfolds as you fight your way through the new Tokyo map, where Shion reigns supreme. Conquer daily and weekly challenges to move across the city, discovering Hashimoto, Yokai, and Subway locations, all littered with lore. The deeper you push into Tokyo, the more you uncover, including choices to make on branching paths and fresh rewards to stack each week. Anima Strike is a narrative driven by momentum, told at your pace, your way.

entry.title: MiG-21 Discounts for the Anniversary of the MiG-21’s First Flight!
entry.content: On June 15th 1955, the MiG-21, one of the most recognizable and mass-produced supersonic fighters in history, took to the skies for the first time. Nearly 11,500 of these aircraft were made. Relatively inexpensive to produce, it was also fast, survivable, and durable, ensuring its popularity worldwide and participation in numerous conflicts. entry.title: Dishonored dev defends loading screens: 'I actually have a soft spot for all that'
entry.content: Raphaël Colantonio, co-creative director of Dishonored, has been streaming a playthrough where he's joined by different members of the team at Arkane who worked on whichever level he's currently replaying. It's been interesting, and has resulted in the unearthing of titbits like Arkane being tapped to make Thief 4 and Blade Runner before they ended up with Dishonored... Read more.

entry.title: If you're bored of shop sims about restocking shelves, here's one about helping someone find the album that could change their life
entry.content: friend of mine who worked in a bookstore used to complain about customers who couldn't remember what they wanted. They'd say unhelpful things like, "I think it had a red cover?" At first the thought of gamifying that only with music instead of books seemed off-putting to me, as someone who goes shopping with overly detailed lists and would sooner bury myself in the ground than ask for help... Read more.
